definition: The Kalecki profit equation (Profits ≡ Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports − Workers' Savings + Capitalist Consumption) provides the formal mechanism for fiscal-inflation persistence. US federal deficits of 6-7% GDP directly sustained corporate profits during 2021-22, creating a channel through which fiscal accommodation perpetuates commodity-driven inflation regardless of monetary policy stance.
evidence: The accounting identity is tautologically correct; US federal deficits of 6-7% GDP directly sustained corporate profits during 2021-22
Rated 'the single most valuable contribution' in pair_3 debate
Post-Keynesian theory well-supports the causal interpretation (deficits → profits → inflation persistence)
No other agent challenged the identity itself; only the direction of causation is contested by mainstream economics
open_questions: Is the causal direction deficits → profits → inflation persistence, or is it reverse/simultaneous?
How does the Kalecki mechanism interact with monetary tightening — can fiscal persistence override rate hikes?
